name: Eddie Heywood
abstract: Edward Heywood Jr. (December 4, 1915 – January 3, 1989) was an American jazz pianist and composer particularly active in the 1940s and 1950s.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Pittsburgh Courier Charity Fund's 7th Annual Concert
Kool-Newport Jazz Festival: Eddie Heywood: Solo Piano

works.t: (They Long to Be) Close to You
Porgy and Bess: Act I, Scene 1. Summertime
Strike Up the Band: The Man I Love

about_quotes.speaker: Zutty Singleton
about_quotes.evidence: Eddie Heywood. See, that's how I opened up. And then later Edridge came in and Clyde Hart came in. And then Don Frye came in. And that's the outfit we brought over to Jimmy Ryans.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: I found Eddie Heywood with a trio, at the Village Vanguard. He didn't have a bass player so I'd sit down there every night.

about_quotes.speaker: Doc Cheatham
about_quotes.evidence: I joined Eddie Heywood at the Cafe Society... Yes. [referring to Eddie Heywood sextet, in '43]

about_quotes.speaker: George Shearing
about_quotes.evidence: Eddie, what was the guy that played “Canadian Sunset,” had such a big hit out of “Canadian Sunset?” ... Yeah, Eddie Heywood on piano [scats]. And Eddie was flying, oh, man. I heard that broadcast. I was in Chicago and I heard that.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Shearing interviewed by Joe Williams, New York City, New York, March 8, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Jonah Jones
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee discusses a third party ('He') who played Eddie Heywood's compositions, but the interviewee does not describe a direct personal or professional relationship with Eddie Heywood.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: Eddie was in there at the time, with that great group he had with Lem Davis and John Simmons and Emmett Berry... That's where I first met all of that gang
